title: PixelSmile: Toward Fine-Grained Facial Expression Editing
task: Fine-grained facial expression editing
key_results: Superior disentanglement of expression semantics; robust identity preservation with continuous, controllable expression editing and smooth blending
comments: The FFE dataset and benchmark are valuable contributions, though the practical applications beyond portrait editing are unclear.

outputs: edited facial images with target expressions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Symmetric joint training with contrastive learning produces more distinguishable expressions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieved precise and stable linear expression control

title: Intern-S1-Pro: Scientific Multimodal Foundation Model at Trillion Scale
task: Scientific multimodal reasoning at trillion-scale
key_results: 1-trillion-parameter model; mastery of 100+ specialized tasks across chemistry, materials, life sciences, earth sciences; outperforms proprietary models in specialized depth
comments: Scale is impressive but claims of outperforming proprietary models lack quantitative evidence in the abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Trillion-scale multimodal models can achieve both general and specialized expertise
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; demonstrated across general benchmarks and 100+ scientific tasks

title: RealRestorer: Towards Generalizable Real-World Image Restoration with Large-Scale Image Editing Models
task: Real-world image restoration with large-scale editing models
key_results: State-of-the-art open-source performance matching closed-source alternatives; covers 9 degradation types; achieves first-rank on RealIR-Bench
comments: The reliance on large-scale pre-trained models may limit generalizability to truly novel degradation types unseen during training.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Large-scale image editing models generalize better to real-world degradations than task-specific restoration models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple degradation types

title: Calibri: Enhancing Diffusion Transformers via Parameter-Efficient Calibration
task: Parameter-efficient calibration of Diffusion Transformers
key_results: ~100 parameter modifications improve DiT performance; reduces inference steps required; maintains high-quality outputs
comments: The evolutionary algorithm approach for optimization is computationally expensive despite claiming efficiency; generalization across different DiT architectures is not fully explored.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Introducing learned scaling parameters in DiT blocks significantly improves generative quality
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; consistent improvements across text-to-image models with minimal parameters

title: MACRO: Advancing Multi-Reference Image Generation with Structured Long-Context Data
task: Multi-reference image generation with structured long-context data
key_results: 400K-sample MacroData dataset with up to 10 references; MacroBench with 4K samples; substantial improvements in multi-reference generation
comments: The structured dataset organization across 4 dimensions is thoughtful, but scalability to handling even more references (15+) remains unaddressed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Structured long-context data with cross-task co-training improves multi-reference generation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; demonstrated synergistic benefits of cross-task training

title: Voxtral TTS
task: Expressive multilingual text-to-speech with voice cloning
key_results: 68.4% win rate over ElevenLabs Flash v2.5 in human evaluation; generates natural speech from 3 seconds of reference audio
comments: The hybrid VQ-FSQ quantization scheme is technically sound, but the 3-second minimum reference requirement may be impractical for very brief voice samples.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hybrid architecture combining semantic tokens (AR) and acoustic tokens (flow-matching) enables expressive multilingual TTS
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; preferred for naturalness and expressivity in human evaluation

title: SlopCodeBench: Benchmarking How Coding Agents Degrade Over Long-Horizon Iterative Tasks
task: Benchmarking code quality degradation in iterative agentic tasks
key_results: No agent solves any problem end-to-end; highest checkpoint solve rate 17.2%; agent code 2.2x more verbose than human; erosion rises in 80% of trajectories
comments: This benchmark exposes a critical gap in current agentic reasoning—code maintenance and refactoring are severely underperforming, indicating fundamental architectural limitations.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current coding agents degrade code quality over iterative extensions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; verbosity increases in 89.8% of trajectories; erosion worsens across iterations

title: MSA: Memory Sparse Attention for Efficient End-to-End Memory Model Scaling to 100M Tokens
task: Efficient long-context memory for LLMs at 100M token scale
key_results: Scales to 100M tokens with <9% degradation from 16K; linear complexity in training/inference; 100M-token inference on 2xA800 GPUs
comments: MSA's approach is sound, but the comparison with RAG systems may not be entirely fair given different architectural paradigms; real-world applicability to domain-specific tasks unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Sparse attention with document-wise RoPE and KV cache compression enables stable 100M-token scaling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves <9% degradation at 100M tokens

title: AVControl: Efficient Framework for Training Audio-Visual Controls
task: Efficient audio-visual control for joint generation models
key_results: State-of-the-art on depth/pose-guided generation; each modality trains in hundreds to thousands of steps; supports diverse modalities with no architectural changes
comments: The parallel canvas design is elegant, but the framework's scalability to even more control modalities (>7) and cross-modal interference remains unexplored.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Parallel canvas approach resolves structural control failures in extended video generation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms baselines on depth, pose, and camera control

title: Less Gaussians, Texture More: 4K Feed-Forward Textured Splatting
task: High-resolution 4K feed-forward 3D view synthesis
key_results: High-fidelity 4K novel view synthesis without per-scene optimization; decouples geometric complexity from resolution; uses fewer Gaussian primitives
comments: The texture prediction approach is practical, but the paper lacks comparison with recent NeRF variants and multi-resolution rendering baselines.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Per-primitive textures decouple resolution scaling from primitive count
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enables 4K synthesis without quadratic primitive growth

title: MuRF: Unlocking the Multi-Scale Potential of Vision Foundation Models
task: Multi-resolution fusion for Vision Foundation Models
key_results: Universal, training-free enhancement working across multiple VFM families (DINOv2, SigLIP2); improves performance on diverse vision tasks
comments: While the approach is elegantly simple, the computational cost of multi-resolution processing is only briefly mentioned; efficiency gains/losses unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-resolution inference fuses complementary global and fine-grained inductive biases
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improves performance across diverse vision tasks in multiple VFM families

title: Representation Alignment for Just Image Transformers is not Easier than You Think
task: Representation alignment for pixel-space Diffusion Transformers
key_results: FID improved from 3.66 to 3.17 for JiT-B/16; IS improved from 275.1 to 284.6; >2x faster convergence; FID=1.81 for JiT-H/16
comments: PixelREPA addresses a real failure mode of REPA for pixel-space diffusion, but the proposed masking strategy seems empirically-driven without theoretical justification.

model_io.model: PixelREPA (alignment target transformation + Masked Transformer Adapter)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Information asymmetry between high-dim image space and compressed semantic target causes REPA failure in JiT
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; PixelREPA mitigates through masked transformer adaptation

title: S2D2: Fast Decoding for Diffusion LLMs via Training-Free Self-Speculation
task: Fast decoding for block-diffusion language models
key_results: Up to 4.7x speedup over autoregressive decoding on SDAR; 1.57x speedup over tuned baseline with +4.5 accuracy points
comments: The self-speculative approach is clever, but overhead of routing policies and verification steps may dominate in latency-critical scenarios.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Same pretrained model can act as both drafter (block-diffusion) and verifier (autoregressive) for efficient decoding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves consistent speedup with improved accuracy across families

title: FinMCP-Bench: Benchmarking LLM Agents for Real-World Financial Tool Use under the Model Context Protocol
task: Benchmarking LLM agents for financial tool use
key_results: 613 samples across 10 scenarios; 65 real financial MCPs; evaluates single-tool, multi-tool, and multi-turn complexity
comments: The benchmark is comprehensive but lacks qualitative error analysis; unclear which failure modes dominate in financial reasoning tasks.

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLMs can effectively invoke financial tools for real-world problem solving
hypotheses.result: Partially tested; FinMCP-Bench establishes standardized evaluation

title: BioVITA: Biological Dataset, Model, and Benchmark for Visual-Textual-Acoustic Alignment
task: Visual-textual-acoustic alignment for species identification
key_results: 1.3M audio clips, 2.3M images covering 14,133 species; unified representation captures species-level semantics beyond taxonomy
comments: The integration of audio is novel for biodiversity tasks, but ecological trait prediction from audio alone may be inherently noisy without companion visual context.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Audio modality provides complementary information for species identification beyond vision and text
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; unified representation captures cross-modal species semantics

title: Revisiting On-Policy Distillation: Empirical Failure Modes and Simple Fixes
task: Improving on-policy distillation for long-horizon LLM training
key_results: Addresses imbalanced signals, unreliable teacher guidance, and tokenizer mismatches; more stable optimization and better downstream performance
comments: The theoretical analysis of variance-bias tradeoffs is insightful, but empirical improvements are incremental; scalability to very long horizons (100+ steps) unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Token-level OPD suffers from information asymmetry; truncated reverse-KL with top-p sampling mitigates this
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improves stability and downstream performance in math and agentic tasks

title: PMT: Plain Mask Transformer for Image and Video Segmentation with Frozen Vision Encoders
task: Efficient image and video segmentation with frozen Vision Foundation Models
key_results: Matches frozen-encoder SOTA on images; ~3x faster; matches fully finetuned performance on video; 8x faster than frozen-encoder baselines
comments: PMT successfully decouples decoder design from encoder training, enabling practical VFM deployment; trade-off between speed and accuracy is well-balanced.

model_io.model: PMT (Plain Mask Decoder on frozen VFM encoders)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Transformer-based decoder can match finetuned performance while keeping encoder frozen for multi-task sharing
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves parity with finetuned methods on video while maintaining encoder frozen

title: MemMA: Coordinating the Memory Cycle through Multi-Agent Reasoning and In-Situ Self-Evolution
task: Coordinating memory construction and retrieval in LLM agents
key_results: Consistent improvements over baselines across LLM backbones; works as plug-and-play enhancement for multiple storage backends
comments: MemMA's multi-agent framework is well-motivated, but the in-situ self-evolution mechanism introduces additional computational overhead during inference.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Coordinating forward (construction/retrieval) and backward (repair) memory paths improves agent performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms baselines across multiple LLM backbones and storage backends

title: Pixel-level Scene Understanding in One Token: Visual States Need What-is-Where Composition
task: Pixel-level scene understanding for robotic decision-making
key_results: State-of-the-art on vision-based robot policy learning; preserves pixel-level scene composition; encodes what-moves-where dynamics
comments: CroBo's global-to-local reconstruction is effective, but applicability to high-dimensional state spaces (100+ objects) is unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Global-to-local reconstruction objective produces visual states encoding semantic identity and spatial configuration
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA on robot policy benchmarks; encodes what-moves-where

title: Vega: Learning to Drive with Natural Language Instructions
task: Language-instructed autonomous driving with world models
key_results: 100K-scene InstructScene dataset; superior planning performance; strong instruction-following abilities
comments: Vega combines vision, language, and world models well, but generalization to out-of-distribution instructions and edge cases remains unvalidated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified vision-language-world-action model enables instruction-following autonomous driving
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; demonstrates superior planning and instruction-following abilities

title: Electrostatic Photoluminescence Tuning in All-Solid-State Perovskite Transistors
task: Electrostatic tuning of photoluminescence in perovskite transistors
key_results: 65-98% modulation of photoluminescence; nearly complete elimination of non-radiative losses possible; high external quantum efficiencies in large-area devices
comments: This materials science work demonstrates impressive optoelectronic control, but practical device stability and long-term performance under continuous operation are not addressed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gate electric field modulates interfacial charge density, controlling radiative vs. non-radiative recombination
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves 65-98% photoluminescence modulation via electrostatic tuning

title: Extending Precipitation Nowcasting Horizons via Spectral Fusion of Radar Observations and Foundation Model Priors
task: Precipitation nowcasting with spectral fusion of radar and weather model priors
key_results: State-of-the-art performance on SEVIR and MeteoNet; extends reliable forecast horizon; maintains structural fidelity
comments: PW-FouCast's frequency-domain fusion is mathematically elegant, but the dependence on external weather foundation model predictions introduces potential error propagation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pangu-Weather spectral priors aligned via frequency modulation improve long-horizon nowcasting
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; extends reliable forecast horizon while maintaining structural fidelity

title: IQuest-Coder-V1 Technical Report
task: Code intelligence with multi-stage training and reasoning
key_results: SOTA performance on agentic software engineering, competitive programming, tool use; recurrent mechanism enables efficient deployment
comments: The code-flow multi-stage training paradigm is comprehensive, but the 'thinking path' utilizing reasoning RL details are vague; comparison with GPT-4 code performance unclear.

model_io.model: IQuest-Coder-V1 (code LLM with reasoning-RL and instruction paths)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Code-flow multi-stage training captures dynamic software logic evolution
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA on agentic, competitive, and tool-use benchmarks

title: Nudging Hidden States: Training-Free Model Steering for Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Large Audio-Language Models
task: Training-free model steering for audio-language reasoning
key_results: Up to 4.4% accuracy improvement over CoT prompting; cross-modal transfer from text to speech effective; high data efficiency
comments: Model steering is a clever inference-time technique, but the cross-modal transfer result is surprising and deserves deeper investigation into why text steering generalizes to speech.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Steering vectors derived from text can guide speech-based reasoning in LALMs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves cross-modal transfer with high data efficiency

title: NorQD AAA+ complex drives metal insertion by a twisting mechanism
task: Elucidate mechanism of metal insertion by AAA+ ATPase NorQ-NorD complex
key_results: Cryo-EM structures reveal two key interfaces between NorQ hexamer and NorD VWA domain; NorQ exerts twisting and stretching force on NorD linker enabling metal insertion into nitric oxide reductase
comments: Elegant structural insight into force generation by AAA+ ATPases, though in vivo validation of metal insertion dynamics would strengthen mechanistic claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: NorQ hexamer applies mechanical force to NorD linker via two distinct interfaces
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by cryo-EM structures and biochemical analyses showing finger-like VWA extension penetrates central pore and C-terminal contacts post-sensor 1 loop

title: A molecular switch in NAC prevents mitochondrial protein mistargeting by SRP
task: Determine mechanism by which NAC detects mitochondrial targeting sequences and prevents SRP mistargeting
key_results: NAC barrel domain adopts stabilized conformation upon MTS exposure; MTS mutations or NAC switch mutations increase barrel dynamics and reduce ribosomal binding; impaired NAC function causes ER stress in human cells
comments: Identifies a novel conformational switch mechanism for protein targeting discrimination, though the structural details of the stabilized barrel state remain to be resolved.

inputs: NAC with functional or mutant switch

hypotheses.hypothesis: NAC switch region enables MTS-dependent barrel stabilization on ribosomes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; switch mutations increase dynamics and reduce ribosomal binding capacity

hypotheses.hypothesis: NAC-mediated prevention of SRP access maintains protein targeting fidelity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; impaired NAC function increases mistargeting and triggers ER stress

title: The hydrodynamic torque dipole from rotary bacterial flagella powers symmetric discs
task: Demonstrate hydrodynamic torque dipole generation by bacterial flagella and force transmission to external objects
key_results: Confined swimming E. coli generate persistent rotation of symmetric discs via torque dipole-mediated traction force; mechanism is contactless and orientation-independent
comments: Novel contactless force transmission mechanism with implications for bacterial transport in confined environments, though the scaling to physiologically relevant pore sizes and biofilm contexts requires further study.

model_io.model: Swimming E. coli with rotary flagella in confined geometry

hypotheses.hypothesis: Torque dipole of confined swimming bacteria can power external disc rotation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; persistent disc rotation observed in confined geometries

hypotheses.hypothesis: Torque dipole force generation is contactless and independent of bacterial orientation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; additive mechanism observed regardless of bacterial orientation

title: 14-3-3γ Knockdown promotes matrix mineralization in human mesenchymal stromal cells
task: Examine 14-3-3γ role in osteoblast differentiation and bone mineralization
key_results: 14-3-3γ knockdown promotes matrix mineralization in human mesenchymal stromal cells
comments: The abstract is incomplete; full mechanistic details, quantitative results, and downstream target confirmation are needed for comprehensive assessment.

hypotheses.hypothesis: 14-3-3γ suppression enhances osteogenic differentiation
hypotheses.result: supported by mineralization phenotype

title: Huntingtin and its allies at the cortico-striatal synapse
task: Review huntingtin pre- and postsynaptic roles in cortico-striatal synapse integrity
key_results: HTT regulates synaptic vesicle cycle at presynaptic terminals and modulates receptor dynamics at postsynaptic density; expanding network of HTT-interacting proteins essential for synaptic structure
comments: This is a comprehensive review synthesizing 30 years of research; the reliance on cell and tissue culture models may not fully capture in vivo disease complexity in HD.

title: Liver kinase B1 maintains natural killer cell survival by regulating redox homeostasis
task: Elucidate LKB1-mediated NK cell survival via redox and iron homeostasis
key_results: Lkb1 ablation impairs autophagy and mitochondrial function, causing ROS-dependent cell death; iron overload exacerbates cytotoxic lipid ROS; ROS/iron inhibition rescues survival; Lkb1 deficiency increases PD-1/TIGIT expression
comments: The coupling of metabolic, oxidative, and iron homeostasis checkpoints is novel, though the AMPK-independent mechanisms remain partially undefined.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lkb1 maintains NK cell survival through metabolic checkpoint regulation independent of AMPK
hypotheses.result: confirmed; AMPK activation or mTORC1 inhibition does not rescue defects

hypotheses.hypothesis: ROS and iron homeostasis coordinately regulate NK cell quiescence
hypotheses.result: confirmed; targeted interventions on ROS or iron rescue survival defects

title: NRIP1 co-activates nuclear translocated FOXO3 to upregulate TFAM expression and promote radioresistance in non-small cell lung cancer
task: Delineate FOXO3/NRIP1-mediated TFAM regulation in NSCLC radioresistance
key_results: TFAM knockdown restores radiosensitivity; FOXO3 directly binds TFAM promoter and NRIP1 acts as coactivator; FOXO3 knockdown suppresses A549-RR tumor growth and improves radiotherapy response in vivo
comments: The FOXO3–NRIP1 coactivator complex is well-characterized, but tissue-specific or context-dependent nuances in this axis may limit generalizability to other NSCLC subtypes.

hypotheses.hypothesis: FOXO3 transcriptionally activates TFAM to confer radioresistance
hypotheses.result: confirmed via ChIP-qPCR and functional knockdown

hypotheses.hypothesis: NRIP1 enhances FOXO3-dependent TFAM regulation as a coactivator
hypotheses.result: confirmed; NRIP1 co-immunoprecipitates with FOXO3 and regulates TFAM without affecting FOXO3 levels

title: MEKK3 bridges gut-brain communication and cerebral cavernous malformation pathogenesis
task: Review gut-brain axis contributions to cerebral cavernous malformation pathogenesis
key_results: Dysbiosis triggers systemic inflammation via LPS-TLR4, SCFAs, and TMAO pathways, affecting cerebrovascular function and blood-brain barrier integrity; gut microbiota may influence endothelial cell proliferation, migration, and apoptosis
comments: This is a conceptual review proposing gut-brain axis mechanisms in CCM; the evidence linking dysbiosis to specific CCM phenotypes remains largely correlative and requires experimental validation.

title: Berberine-entrapped albumin nanoparticles ameliorate chemically induced liver injury by restoring oxidative balance and autophagic-apoptotic crosstalk
task: Evaluate berberine-nanoparticle hepatoprotection via oxidative balance and autophagy-apoptosis crosstalk
key_results: BRB-BSA NPs reduce serum uric acid (0.20±0.07 vs 1.35±0.1 mg/dL in DEN/CCl₄), restore SOD activity, normalize autophagic signaling via PI3K/mTOR/p62/LC3 downregulation/upregulation, and promote apoptosis (elevated Bax, reduced Bcl-2) with near-complete hepatic recovery
comments: The post-injury treatment superiority over prophylaxis is noteworthy, and the multi-pathway restoration is comprehensive, though in vivo translational efficacy and long-term safety require further assessment.

hypotheses.hypothesis: BRB-BSA NPs restore oxidative-autophagic-apoptotic homeostasis in DEN/CCl₄ injury
hypotheses.result: confirmed; post-injury treatment more effective than prophylaxis

hypotheses.hypothesis: PI3K inhibition underlies BRB-mediated autophagy normalization
hypotheses.result: supported by molecular docking and pathway analysis

title: Chromatin dynamics of the Klf4 locus in mouse pluripotent cells
task: Investigate chromatin dynamics of Klf4 locus in relation to transcriptional activity
key_results: ANCHOR3 tracking reveals homogeneous chromatin motion of cis-regulatory elements and non-regulatory regions in both transcribing mESCs and non-transcribing EpiLCs
comments: The finding challenges the transcription-driven locus dynamics model, suggesting that other mechanisms or larger-scale chromatin organization may dominate over local transcriptional activity.

inputs: ANCHOR3 DNA labeling at multiple Klf4 locus positions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Active transcription drives differential chromatin dynamics across regulatory regions
hypotheses.result: not supported; homogeneous motion observed in both transcribing and non-transcribing cells

title: Differential miRNA expression during replicative senescence of dental pulp stem cells with potential for forensic age assessment
task: Identify miRNA biomarkers of replicative senescence in dental pulp stem cells
key_results: Senescence induction increased miR-433-5p, miR-331-5p, miR-221-5p, miR-328-5p and decreased miR-205-5p, miR-455-5p, miR-21-5p; combined miRNA panels significantly improved discrimination of senescence states
comments: The proof-of-concept for miRNA panels is solid, but forensic applicability requires validation across broader donor cohorts and post-mortem conditions.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Individual miRNAs serve as predictive biomarkers of senescence
hypotheses.result: partially supported; individual miRNAs show moderate predictive ability

hypotheses.hypothesis: miRNA panels improve senescence discrimination
hypotheses.result: confirmed; multivariable models combining miRNAs greatly enhance predictive power

title: The fluoroquinoline compound exerts anti-erythroleukemic effects by dual-targeting GLUT1 and the PI3K/AKT signaling pathway
task: Inhibit erythroleukemia proliferation via dual GLUT1 and PI3K/AKT targeting
key_results: FKL-137 significantly inhibits cell proliferation and induces apoptosis in vitro; prevents Friend virus-induced erythroleukemia and splenomegaly in mice
comments: Dual targeting of glucose metabolism and signaling is therapeutically promising but whether GLUT1 inhibition is the primary driver versus PI3K/AKT remains unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: GLUT1 inhibition coupled with PI3K/AKT suppression drives anti-tumor effects
hypotheses.result: confirmed via mechanistic studies showing reduced glucose metabolism and altered signaling

title: Multi-omics and network pharmacology reveal the mechanisms of Scutellaria barbata D.Don and Scleromitrion diffusum (Willd.) R.J.Wang against pancreatic cancer
task: Dissect multi-omics mechanisms of SB-SD herbal formula against pancreatic cancer
key_results: SB-SD inhibits PC cell proliferation, promotes apoptosis, and induces S/G2 arrest via HSP90AA1/MAPK3; suppresses PANC-1 xenograft growth; multi-omics reveal microbiota-metabolite-protein network involving Bacteroides caccae, Lactobacillus, choline metabolism, and BCKDK/GATM
comments: The integration of multi-omics with gut microbiota is comprehensive, but causality between microbiota changes and tumor suppression remains correlative rather than mechanistically proven.

hypotheses.hypothesis: SB-SD suppresses PC through HSP90AA1/MAPK3 pathway modulation
hypotheses.result: confirmed via Western blotting in cell and xenograft models

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gut microbiota and metabolite shifts contribute to antitumor effects
hypotheses.result: supported by metagenomic and metabolomic profiling; mechanistic link requires validation

title: Endogenous and environmental factors that induce DNA replication defects and genomic instability in ER-negative heterozygous BRCA1 cells
task: Investigate estrogen-induced DNA replication defects and genomic instability in BRCA1-mutant cells
key_results: Estrogen and estrogen metabolites inhibit replication fork progression and trigger DNA breaks, deletions, and LOH in BRCA1mut/+ cells; atrazine herbicide similarly induces instability; indole-3-carbinol (I3C) prevents estrogen-induced DNA damage
comments: The identification of dietary compound I3C as a protective agent is promising but requires clinical validation; the mechanism by which estrogen metabolites specifically target BRCA1-deficient cells needs deeper mechanistic investigation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Estrogen and metabolites are genotoxic for ER-negative BRCA1-mutant cells
hypotheses.result: confirmed; estrogen triggers DNA breaks and cancer-initiating mutations

hypotheses.hypothesis: I3C prevents estrogen-induced DNA damage in BRCA1-mutant cells
hypotheses.result: confirmed; I3C mitigates replication stress and genomic instability

title: PACS1 syndrome mutation disrupts dynein-mediated cargo transport via HDAC6 and BICD2
task: Characterize PACS1R203W-induced dynein dysfunction via HDAC6 and BICD2
key_results: PACS1R203W induces dynein loss-of-function via HDAC6-BICD2 complex formation; reduces dynein initiation frequency and velocity; defects rescued by HDAC6 inhibition or Lis1 expression; impairs furin trafficking and Golgi organization
comments: The identification of PACS1–HDAC6–BICD2 as a pathogenic complex provides a mechanistic link between PACS1 syndrome and microtubule trafficking disorders, though broader implications for similar neurodevelopmental syndromes remain to be explored.

model_io.model: PACS1R203W cells + HDAC6 inhibitor or Lis1
outputs: restored dynein initiation frequency and velocity

hypotheses.hypothesis: PACS1R203W–HDAC6–BICD2 complex disrupts dynein function
hypotheses.result: confirmed; cargo motility assays show reduced initiation frequency and velocity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dynein dysfunction underlies Golgi fragmentation in PACS1 syndrome
hypotheses.result: supported; HDAC6 inhibition and Lis1 expression rescue dynein and Golgi phenotypes

title: ALDOB K87 lactylation drives mitochondrial fission and metabolic reprogramming in pulmonary hypertension
task: Characterize lactate-driven mitochondrial fission and metabolic reprogramming in pulmonary hypertension
key_results: ALDOB-K87 lactylation amplifies glycolytic flux and recruits DRP1 to mitochondria via SENP3-mediated deSUMOylation; pharmacological suppression of lactylation attenuates PH progression in vivo
comments: The lactate–ALDOB–DRP1 axis represents a novel metabolic-mitochondrial feedback loop, though the physiological relevance of lactylation versus phosphorylation needs clarification.

model_io.model: hypoxic PASMCs and rodent PH models

hypotheses.hypothesis: ALDOB lactylation drives DRP1 recruitment and mitochondrial fission
hypotheses.result: confirmed; DRP1 deSUMOylation by SENP3 is mechanistically required

hypotheses.hypothesis: SIRT1 downregulation sustains pathological lactylation
hypotheses.result: confirmed; SIRT1 acts as delactylase for ALDOB

title: SMART: spatial multi-omic aggregation using graph neural networks and metric learning
task: Spatial multi-omics data integration across tissue domains
key_results: SMART accurately identifies spatial regions of anatomical structures; SMART-MS extends integration across multiple tissue sections with exceptional computational efficiency
comments: Framework demonstrates modularity and scalability but validation on diverse tissue types and modality combinations would strengthen claims.

model_io.model: SMART (Spatial Multi-omic Aggregation with GNN and metric learning)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Graph neural networks with triplet metric learning can effectively integrate spatial multi-omics data
hypotheses.result: SMART accurately identifies anatomical structures and scales to large datasets

title: Mining of food metagenomes reveals an unexplored diversity of dsDNA bacteriophages
task: Characterization of bacteriophage diversity in food metagenomes and virus-host interactions
key_results: FVGC comprises ~3400 metagenome-assembled viruses (MAVs) from >3000 food metagenomes with 91 novel Caudoviricetes lineages; linked MAVs to ESKAPE pathogens; minimal phage-mediated AMR dissemination (<10% of MAVs)
comments: Valuable resource for viral ecology, though functional validation of predicted host interactions and cross-domain transferability remain open.

model_io.model: Metagenomic viral assembly and taxonomic classification

hypotheses.hypothesis: Extensive uncharacterized viral diversity exists in food systems with predictable host-pathogen associations
hypotheses.result: FVGC identified 91 novel lineages; linked MAVs to clinically relevant pathogens; minimal phage-mediated AMR

title: From FAIR to CURE: guidelines for computational models of biological systems
task: Framework for ensuring credibility, reproducibility and extensibility of computational biological models
key_results: CURE principles proposed: Credible (verification/validation/uncertainty quantification), Understandable (model clarity/annotation), Reproducible (standards/open science), Extensible (modular code/open standards)
comments: Timely and necessary framework, though implementation automation challenges and discipline-specific nuances merit further discussion.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Computational models require complementary guidelines beyond FAIR data principles for biomedical trust
hypotheses.result: CURE framework proposed with baseline and recommended requirements for model management and reuse

title: High-resolution metagenome assembly for modern long reads with myloasm
task: High-resolution metagenome assembly for modern long reads enabling recovery of within-species diversity
key_results: Myloasm assembled 3× more complete circular contigs than next-best assembler on ONT metagenomes; recovered six complete Prevotella copri and eight complete TM7 (Saccharibacteria) single-contig genomes; ONT assemblies comparable to HiFi
comments: Significant algorithmic advance leveraging polymorphic k-mers and differential abundance; within-species diversity recovery opens new ecological insights.

model_io.model: myloasm (metagenome assembler with polymorphic k-mers and abundance-guided graph simplification)
inputs: long-read sequencing data (PacBio HiFi, ONT R10.4)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Polymorphic k-mers and differential abundance enable high-resolution metagenome assembly comparable to HiFi on ONT data
hypotheses.result: Myloasm assembled 3× more complete circular contigs; recovered previously inaccessible within-species diversity (6 complete P. copri, 8 complete TM7 contigs)

title: 3d-OT: a deep geometry-aware framework for heterogeneous slices alignment of spatial multi-omics
task: 3D alignment of heterogeneous spatial multi-omics slices with nonrigid deformations
key_results: 3d-OT captures anatomical details of mouse brain cortex layers and constructs 3D spatiotemporal trajectories of embryonic development; outperforms existing methods in nonrigid deformation tracking
comments: Novel use of optimal transport for alignment is elegant, but chamfer distance evaluation may benefit from biological validation metrics.

model_io.model: 3d-OT (deep geometry-aware framework with soft correspondence optimal transport)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Soft correspondence optimal transport can resolve nonrigid deformations in spatial multi-omics alignment
hypotheses.result: Successfully aligns mouse brain, heart, and neural crest tissues and reconstructs 3D embryonic development trajectory

title: A digital biomarker dataset from hematopoietic cell transplant caregivers and patients
task: Physiological and psychological biomarker dataset from hematopoietic cell transplant caregiver-patient dyads
key_results: Comprehensive dataset of 166 HCT dyads over 120 days post-transplant including minute-level heart rate, daily sleep/steps, mood scores, PROMIS® questionnaires, and clinical outcomes
comments: Rich paired dataset enabling investigation of interdependencies, though analysis of caregiver-patient physiological synchrony and predictive modeling of complications would add analytical value.

model_io.model: Integrated wearable and clinical data capture system

title: A dataset of insect sounds from 459 species for bioacoustic machine learning
task: Large-scale insect sound dataset for acoustic biodiversity monitoring via deep learning
key_results: InsectSet459 comprises 26,298 audio files (226.6 hours) from 459 species (310 Orthoptera, 149 Cicadidae); benchmark deep learning classifiers show good performance with room for improvement on broad-frequency acoustic signals
comments: Valuable dataset addressing data scarcity in insect bioacoustics, though classifier performance gaps (likely due to variable sample rates) suggest methodological advances needed.

inputs: insect sound audio (variable sample rates, broad frequency ranges)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Large-scale labeled insect sound dataset enables development of robust deep learning classifiers for biodiversity monitoring
hypotheses.result: InsectSet459 benchmarked; good baseline performance achieved with identified potential for improvement in handling variable frequencies/sample rates

title: A novel superpixel based Vision Transformer for improving interpretability in glaucoma screening
task: Superpixel-based Vision Transformer for interpretable glaucoma screening
key_results: SpxViT achieves 91.9% accuracy (vs ViT-B/16 92.5%) with improved interpretability; attention maps focus on clinically relevant optic disc/cup regions
comments: Addresses important interpretability challenge in medical imaging, though quantitative comparison of attention map clinical relevance versus standard ViTs would strengthen claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Superpixel-based tokenization preserves semantic boundaries for improved interpretability over grid-based tokenization
hypotheses.result: SpxViT achieves comparable accuracy (91.9%) with more clinically consistent attention maps focused on optic disc/cup

title: KM-DBSCAN: an enhanced density and centroid based border detection framework for data reduction towards green AI
task: Data reduction via hybrid K-Means/DBSCAN clustering for green AI model training
key_results: KM-DBSCAN achieves up to 90% data reduction, training speedups 3.6× to 6900×, carbon emissions 0.0219-5.374g, while preserving competitive accuracy (90.39% melanoma with 28.7% data, 0.0061% loss, 71.65% emission reduction)
comments: Practical green AI approach combining clustering benefits, though computational cost of clustering itself and real-world carbon accounting methodology warrant clarification.

inputs: training datasets (Banana, USPS, Adult9a, Collision, Dry Bean, Melanoma)
outputs: reduced dataset with intelligent sample selection

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hybrid K-Means/DBSCAN clustering enables significant data reduction while preserving model accuracy and reducing training carbon emissions
hypotheses.result: Up to 90% data reduction with 3.6× to 6900× speedups and 71.65% carbon emission reduction on melanoma with minimal accuracy loss

title: Graph attention network-based multimodal approach for lung diseases classification
task: Multimodal lung disease classification from clinical text and chest X-rays
key_results: Graph Attention Network-based approach achieves 95.73% accuracy, 95.75% precision, 95.72% recall, 95.70% F1-score with ECE 0.0124 on multiclass lung disease classification
comments: Strong performance metrics, though generalization to external datasets and attention map clinical validation would strengthen clinical applicability claims.

model_io.model: Graph Attention Network with Clinical ModernBERT and RAD-DINO

hypotheses.hypothesis: Token-level cross-modal interactions via GAT can integrate imaging and textual information for lung disease classification
hypotheses.result: 95.73% accuracy on multiclass lung disease classification with robust attention maps focused on optic disc/cup

title: Structural error asymmetry and harm-weighted analysis of ChatGPT versus ICU Physicians in acid–base interpretation: a prospective observational study
task: Harm-weighted error analysis of ChatGPT versus ICU physicians in acid-base interpretation
key_results: Overall accuracy 82% (physicians) vs 72% (ChatGPT); mixed disorder sensitivity 0.96 vs 0.63; ChatGPT produced 16.7% false-normal classifications (false reassurance); harm-weighted error significantly higher for ChatGPT (p=0.026)
comments: Rigorous complexity-stratified and harm-weighted evaluation reveals critical safety gaps masked by aggregate metrics, setting exemplar for clinical AI assessment.

inputs: arterial blood gas (ABG) data from 50 ICU patients

hypotheses.hypothesis: ChatGPT and ICU physicians show equivalent overall diagnostic accuracy in acid-base interpretation
hypotheses.result: Aggregate agreement comparable (κ=0.63 vs 0.73) but structural differences in mixed disorder sensitivity and false-reassurance rate reveal safety concerns

title: Differential microRNA expression profiles and predicted miRNA–mRNA regulatory networks in human macrophage-like cells infected with Leishmania infantum
task: miRNA-mRNA regulatory network analysis in Leishmania infantum-infected macrophages
key_results: 24 and 10 dysregulated miRNAs at 24h; 25 and 12 at 48h post-infection; high fraction of dysregulated transcription factors (TFs) predicted as miRNA targets in cholesterol/lipid, VEGF, and NF2EL2 pathways
comments: Comprehensive small RNA-seq study with targeted pathway focus, though functional validation of predicted miRNA-mRNA interactions remains needed.

model_io.model: Small RNA-seq and differential expression analysis
inputs: U937 cells infected with L. infantum at 24h and 48h
outputs: dysregulated miRNAs (24 up, 10 down at 24h; 25 up, 12 down at 48h)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dysregulated miRNAs in L. infantum infection target transcription factors and metabolic pathway genes
hypotheses.result: High fraction of TFs predicted as miRNA targets; dysregulation impacts cholesterol, lipid, VEGF, and NF2EL2 pathways

title: Nonlinear dynamics of Nosema ceranae and the fragile resilience of honeybee colonies under environmental strain
task: Nonlinear dynamics modeling of Nosema ceranae persistence and outbreak cycles in honeybee colonies
key_results: SIRS model identifies transcritical bifurcation (disease eradication→endemic) and Hopf bifurcation (sustained oscillations from resource limitation); bistability indicates small perturbations can trigger large-amplitude outbreaks
comments: Rigorous mathematical framework explaining cyclical disease prevalence, though empirical validation of predicted bifurcation thresholds in field colonies would enhance applicability.

model_io.model: Nonlinear SIRS model with susceptibility renewal and resource saturation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Partial recovery and resource limitation drive complex epidemic patterns in Nosema ceranae
hypotheses.result: Model identifies transcritical and Hopf bifurcations explaining endemic persistence and cyclical outbreaks; bistability demonstrates sensitivity to interventions

title: Towards a more reliable assessment of aortic diameters using a Bayesian Z-score
task: Bayesian Z-score framework for reliable aortic diameter assessment accounting for epistemic uncertainty
key_results: Bayesian approach detects more dilatations in at-risk patients; identifies uncertain cases via epistemic uncertainty quantification; trained on 1,947 healthy subjects
comments: Methodological advance addressing overlooked epistemic uncertainty in clinical metrics, though validation on larger prospective cohorts is needed.

model_io.model: Bayesian heteroscedastic Gaussian process regression for aortic diameter

hypotheses.hypothesis: Bayesian reformulation of Z-score captures aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty in aortic diameter assessment
hypotheses.result: Enhanced detection of dilatations and identification of uncertain cases for more reliable clinical decision-making

title: AI-driven body composition atlas reveals its association with NSCLC immunotherapy outcome and molecular background: a multicenter study
task: AI-driven body composition analysis and association with immunotherapy outcomes in NSCLC
key_results: 92 body composition parameters extracted from CT with ICC >0.87; intermuscular fat volume (males) and T12 subcutaneous fat density (females) predict survival; sex-specific associations with interferon/macrophage pathways in 2,132 patients (8 cohorts)
comments: Comprehensive multicenter study with gender-stratified mechanistic insights, though causal pathway validation via intervention studies would strengthen recommendations.

model_io.model: Deep learning CT image segmentation and parameter extraction
inputs: computed tomography images from NSCLC patients (n=2,132)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Body composition parameters predict immunotherapy outcomes through sex-specific immune mechanisms
hypotheses.result: High IMFV (males) correlates with upregulated interferon pathways and lower CD8+ T cell exhaustion; high T12 fat density (females) with M1 macrophage polarization

title: Overcoming Data Loss in Wearable Disease Detection with GAN-Based Imputation
task: GAN-based imputation of missing wearable sensor data for disease detection
key_results: System detects 100 early infection alerts (42 solely from imputation) with 11.9-day lead time matching parasitemia window; 35% improvement in early detection despite 50% data coverage; cross-pathogen generalization with 58% error reduction
comments: Pragmatic approach for low-resource settings, though clinical validation on larger cohorts and comparison with other imputation methods would strengthen evidence.

model_io.model: Lightweight GAN framework for heart rate imputation
inputs: partial wearable heart rate time series (50% coverage)

hypotheses.hypothesis: GAN trained on external COVID-19 data can generalize to malaria detection in rural populations
hypotheses.result: Alerts triggered 11.9 days before symptom onset, with 35% improved early detection sensitivity

title: Scaling and quantization of large-scale foundation model enables resource-efficient predictions in network biology
task: Resource-efficient foundation model scaling for transcriptional analysis
key_results: Model quantization reduces fine-tuning time to 15% and memory to 34% of full-precision model while preserving performance on ~10^4 million single-cell transcriptomes
comments: Demonstrates practical utility of quantization for biomedical foundation models, though biological knowledge preservation claims require deeper mechanistic validation.

model_io.model: Scaled and quantized foundation model for network biology
inputs: ~104 million human single-cell transcriptomes from diverse tissues/diseases

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quantization preserves contextual embedding spaces of full-precision models
hypotheses.result: Zero-shot and fine-tuning performance matches full model with 15% time and 34% memory overhead

title: HorusEye: a self-supervised foundation model for generalizable X-ray tomography restoration
task: Self-supervised X-ray tomography image restoration across modalities
key_results: HorusEye trained on 100+ million images generalizes across modalities and degradation types, improving photon efficiency and high-frequency recovery; clinical studies show enhanced lesion detectability
comments: Foundation model approach is promising for image restoration, though cross-modality generalization claims would benefit from explicit domain shift quantification.

model_io.model: HorusEye (self-supervised foundation model with interslice contrastive pretraining)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Contrastive pretraining on raw data enables learning of realistic degradation processes without paired supervision
hypotheses.result: HorusEye generalizes to diverse modalities and unseen imaging modes, outperforming task-specific restoration methods

title: Generative AI for misalignment-resistant virtual staining to accelerate histopathology workflows
task: Virtual staining for histopathology via cascaded registration
key_results: 23.8% improvement in image quality for highly misaligned samples; pathologists achieve 52% accuracy distinguishing virtual from chemical stains
comments: Elegant decoupling of generation and registration enables real-world imperfectly-aligned data; 52% indistinguishability is impressive for clinical adoption.

model_io.model: Generative AI with cascaded registration mechanism

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cascaded registration mechanism enables virtual staining from imperfectly paired data
hypotheses.result: 23.8% quality improvement for misaligned samples; pathologist indistinguishability at 52%

title: Supernetwork-based efficient mapping of deep learning applications to mixed-precision hardware using model adaptation
task: Mapping deep learning models to mixed-precision hardware
key_results: ~2.2× faster mappings, ~3.4% accuracy improvement over fully analog approach, 80% of model weights mapped to analog hardware
comments: The approach elegantly combines hardware-aware architecture search with mixed-precision quantization but evaluation is limited to standard benchmarks.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mapping-aware adaptation enables efficient deployment on heterogeneous accelerators
hypotheses.result: Achieved 2.2× speedup and maintained accuracy with 80% analog weight mapping

title: Multimodal and Hyperspectral Dataset for Segmentation of Bulky Waste using VIS, IR, NIR, and Terahertz Imaging
task: Multimodal waste segmentation dataset creation
key_results: WoodVIT: 56 multi-sensor scenes, 22,659 annotated patches, binary wood/non-wood classification with 16 subclass labels
comments: Comprehensive multimodal dataset with practical waste-sorting applications, but baseline CNN results not explicitly reported.

model_io.model: Multimodal sensor fusion (VIS, NIR, IR, THz)

title: Ensemble-based detection of distributed denial-of-service attacks in IoT networks using majority decision mechanisms
task: Ensemble DDoS attack detection in IoT networks
key_results: Majority voting achieves 99.87-100% accuracy on DNS, NetBIOS, LDAP, UDP, SNMP attacks; significantly reduces false positives
comments: Pragmatic ensemble approach with practical applicability to resource-constrained IoT; preprocessing and feature selection are well-designed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ensemble majority voting reduces false positives while maintaining detection accuracy
hypotheses.result: 99.87-100% accuracy with minimal false positives on CICDDOS2019

title: ScaleMamba-YOLO: a multi-scale MambaYOLO for medical object detection
task: Multi-scale lesion detection in medical images
key_results: AP scores: Br35H 72.7%, BCCD 65.0%, PLoPy 85.7%, VOC0712 64.6%; consistent 1.7-2.3% improvement over MambaYOLO
comments: ScaleMamba-YOLO effectively addresses variable lesion scales with selective state-space modeling, though improvements are incremental.

inputs: Medical images (chest X-rays, blood cell, tumor)

hypotheses.hypothesis: MMLFE-Block with multi-kernel convolution improves multi-scale lesion detection
hypotheses.result: Consistent 1.7-2.3% AP improvement over baseline MambaYOLO

title: Low-data cross-modal adaptation for remote sensing with proxy-enhanced multi-granularity feature caching
task: Cross-modal adaptation for remote sensing with vision-language models
key_results: Stable few-shot performance where fine-tuning fails; outperforms existing cross-modal adaptation methods on multiple benchmarks
comments: Well-designed framework combining LLM-augmented prompts, proxy calibration, and feature caching; particularly strong in low-data regimes.

model_io.model: Proxy-enhanced multi-granularity feature caching with CLIP

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLM-augmented prompts and proxy-based support sets improve semantic alignment
hypotheses.result: Stable few-shot performance and outperformed existing methods

title: A physics-informed machine learning framework for predicting and mitigating doxorubicin nanocarrier toxicity in normal cells
task: Physics-informed machine learning for nanocarrier toxicity prediction
key_results: PINN achieves R² 0.89, RMSE 0.14; identifies optimal design space (120-150 nm size, -25 to -35 mV zeta potential); validation errors <3%
comments: Hybrid PIML approach effectively incorporates domain constraints; experimental validation on independent studies demonstrates practical utility.

hypotheses.hypothesis: PINN incorporating drug release kinetics and diffusion constraints improves predictions
hypotheses.result: R² 0.89; experimental validation errors <3%

title: Enhancing credit card fraud detection with a hybrid approach using machine and deep learning
task: Credit card fraud detection with hybrid ensemble methods
key_results: Proposed stacking ensemble achieves accuracy/precision/recall/F1/AUC all reaching 1.0 or near-perfect; 37 models evaluated
comments: Comprehensive evaluation with multiple sampling techniques and interpretability methods; perfect metrics suggest potential data leakage or overfitting risk.

model_io.model: Stacking ensemble (ET, CNN, LSTM, XGBoost) or (ET, AdaBoost, XGBoost)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ensemble methods with data balancing improve fraud detection
hypotheses.result: Stacking ensembles achieve near-perfect performance (AUC 1.0)

title: Comparative optimization of overcurrent relay coordination in DG-integrated distribution networks: water cycle algorithm versus genetic algorithm and big bang–big crunch
task: Overcurrent relay coordination in DG-integrated distribution networks
key_results: Comparative evaluation of WCA, GA, and BB-BC algorithms for OCR coordination; all algorithms achieve coordinated settings under multiple operating modes
comments: Thorough comparative study but lacks explicit performance rankings; unclear which algorithm is superior for practical deployment.

title: Enhancing IELTS writing automated scoring with M-LoRA fine-tuned LLAMA-3 and human feedback-driven PPO reinforcement learning
task: Automated IELTS essay scoring and feedback generation
key_results: Significant improvements in essay scoring accuracy and feedback quality using M-LoRA fine-tuning and PPO reinforcement learning on 5,088 expert-annotated essays
comments: The paper demonstrates practical application of LLMs to educational assessment but lacks specific quantitative metrics (e.g., correlation with human raters).

outputs: Scores on Task Response, Coherence/Cohesion, Lexical Resource, Grammatical Range/Accuracy

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-task supervised fine-tuning prevents catastrophic forgetting in scoring tasks
hypotheses.result: Successfully captured features across four IELTS scoring dimensions

title: Enhanced control of continuous stirred tank reactor with two-degree-of-freedom PID driven by Kirchhoff’s law algorithm
task: Temperature control in continuous stirred tank reactor
key_results: KLA-tuned 2DOF-PID achieves faster rise/settling times and minimal steady-state error vs. other optimizers; demonstrates robust control of nonlinear thermal system
comments: Physics-based optimization (KLA) is well-motivated for nonlinear CSTR; comparative evaluation limited to standard optimizers.

model_io.model: 2DOF-PID controller optimized by Kirchhoff's Law Algorithm

hypotheses.hypothesis: KLA-optimized 2DOF-PID improves robustness and accuracy for nonlinear thermal systems
hypotheses.result: Faster response and minimal steady-state error vs. other optimizers

title: Prototype-oriented contrastive mean-teacher for unsupervised domain adaptive object detection
task: Unsupervised domain adaptation for object detection
key_results: PoCoMT achieves state-of-the-art performance through prototype-contrastive learning; improves discriminative abilities beyond cross-domain transferability
comments: Novel integration of contrastive learning, prototype learning, and mean-teacher self-training is well-motivated; extensive validation across benchmarks.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Prototype Alignment Network reduces intra- and inter-domain contrastive losses
hypotheses.result: Achieves state-of-the-art UDA-OD performance

title: A novel quantum convolutional neural network framework for quantum-enhanced classification of pixelated colour images
task: Quantum convolutional neural networks for image classification
key_results: Six-class validation accuracy 82.05% vs. classical CNN 40.00%; binary classification: No-QCNN 89.7% vs. classical CNN 100%
comments: Demonstrates QCNN advantage in low-data multiclass settings but performance degrades with dataset size due to NISQ limitations; complementary, not superior, paradigm.

model_io.model: Novel Quantum CNN (No-QCNN) with Variational Quantum Classifier

hypotheses.hypothesis: QCNN with ZZFeatureMap encoding improves generalization in low-data multiclass settings
hypotheses.result: 82.05% validation accuracy vs. 40% classical CNN on 50-image dataset

title: Domain knowledge-integrated reinforcement learning control of nonlinear tunable vibration absorber under nonstationary excitation
task: Reinforcement learning control of tunable vibration absorber
key_results: Modified Q-learning achieves R² 0.994 vs. optimal baseline; up to 58% vibration energy reduction under nonstationary multi-frequency excitation
comments: Effective integration of vibration-domain knowledge (Parseval's theorem) reduces RL complexity; real-time experimental validation is strong.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Domain knowledge integration improves RL convergence and reduces computational complexity
hypotheses.result: R² 0.994; 58% vibration energy reduction under nonstationary excitation

title: Keeping up with the regions: a hybrid machine learning framework for estimating regional input-output tables
task: Regional input-output table estimation
key_results: GAN+Boost achieves R² improvements of 8.9 pp (NIOT) and 1.2 pp (WIOT); diagonal correlation improves 160% on NIOT; Gini metrics show 73-82% better preservation
comments: Novel integration of IPF constraints within GAN generator is creative, though the modest WIOT improvement suggests dataset-dependent performance.

model_io.model: GAN with IPF layer + residual boosting

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hard marginal constraints via IPF layer improve GAN-based IO table generation
hypotheses.result: Significantly outperformed improved RAS baseline on both NIOT and WIOT

title: Comparative behavior of steam turbine model for dynamical power system analyses by means of multiple fractional and artificial neural network techniques
task: Steam turbine dynamic modeling and neural network prediction
key_results: ANN-based predictive model with comparison of fractional differential techniques; validation via transfer function analysis
comments: Combines fractional calculus and ANN for turbine modeling but lacks explicit quantitative performance metrics or accuracy comparisons.

model_io.model: Artificial Neural Network with Laplace/Sumudu transforms

title: Network topology and recovery delay thresholds determine cascading failure vulnerability in sports systems
task: Network topology effects on cascading failure in sports systems
key_results: Scale-free networks 57% more vulnerable than regular (NVI 1.24 vs. 0.79); immediate recovery (r_t=1) prevents cascading; delayed recovery (r_t≥2) triggers exponential propagation
comments: Rigorous network-agent modeling with empirical validation; phase space analysis reveals bistability important for management.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Scale-free topology increases cascading failure vulnerability due to star-player dependence
hypotheses.result: 57% higher vulnerability (NVI 1.24) vs. regular networks

title: Gender shapes the relationship between productivity and journal prestige in science
task: Gender disparities in productivity-prestige trajectories in academia
key_results: Male researchers overrepresented in hyperprolific region; female peers achieve comparable or higher journal prestige; gender-discipline interactions observed
comments: Sophisticated Bayesian hierarchical modeling on 6000+ elite Brazilian researchers; findings suggest structural barriers rather than merit differences.

model_io.model: Bayesian hierarchical modeling of career trajectories

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gender shapes the relationship between productivity and journal prestige
hypotheses.result: Male researchers follow productivity-oriented paths; female peers prioritize prestige; discipline-specific patterns observed

title: Design optimization and stiffness-equivalent method for an integrated starter generator in aerospace applications
task: Integrated starter generator optimization and vibration analysis
key_results: Stiffness-equivalent method validated via 3D FEA; optimization achieves lightweight design meeting operational constraints
comments: Solid engineering methodology combining finite element analysis with optimization, but limited to aerospace starter generators; generalization potential unclear.

title: A unified deep learning framework integrating OpenStreetMap for multi-domain urban planning tasks
task: Multi-domain urban planning with satellite and OSM data
key_results: Land-use: 91.6% accuracy; building footprints: 94.0% accuracy; traffic: RMSE 3.6 veh/h; air quality: RMSE 2.3 µg/m³
comments: Well-executed multi-task framework but evaluation limited to single city (Krasnodar); generalization potential unclear.

title: PCB-YOLOV8X: a network for detecting micro-sized defects on PCB surfaces based on enhanced feature information
task: PCB surface micro-defect detection
key_results: mAP@0.5 98.44%, Precision 97.67%, Recall 96.34%; improvements of 2.76%, 2.33%, 2.59% over YOLOv8
comments: Effective use of adaptive feature extraction and loss function optimization for micro-defect detection; incremental but solid improvements.

hypotheses.hypothesis: IWD-CIoU loss improves matching between predictions and ground truth
hypotheses.result: 98.44% mAP@0.5 with 2.76% improvement over YOLOv8

title: Advanced channel estimation in OTFS and NOMA using deep bayesian gaussian processes and compressive sensing
task: Channel estimation in OTFS-NOMA systems
key_results: NMSE 0.01447 at 12 dB (90% lower than MMSE); BER 0.021159 at 12 dB; 50% reduction in pilot overhead
comments: Strong performance in high-mobility scenarios; combines deep learning with uncertainty quantification effectively.

hypotheses.hypothesis: DBGP-CS with compressive sensing reduces pilot overhead by 50%
hypotheses.result: NMSE 0.01447 at 12 dB with 100 users at 120 km/h

title: Dismantling complex networks based on higher-order graph neural network
task: Network dismantling using higher-order graph neural networks
key_results: SPR model integrates multi-dimensional higher-order relations; demonstrates superior dismantling efficiency on real-world and synthetic networks
comments: Theoretical framework is robust but specific quantitative improvements on benchmark datasets are not detailed.

model_io.model: Higher-order Graph Neural Network (HoGNN) with SPR dismantling

hypotheses.hypothesis: Integration of macro/micro higher-order relations improves key node identification
hypotheses.result: Superior dismantling efficiency with minimal target removals

title: Room temperature collective blinking and photon bunching from CsPbBr3 quantum dot superlattice
task: Demonstrating room-temperature collective photon bunching in perovskite quantum dot superlattices
key_results: Collective blinking and photon bunching (degree ~3.9) at room temperature; long-range exciton migration to nanoscale energy traps; biexciton-exciton cascade emission mechanism confirmed via power-dependent analysis
comments: Room-temperature collective effects are notable, but the origin of energy traps (defects vs intentional superlattice design) and their reproducibility across samples warrant deeper investigation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Long-range exciton migration in superlattices creates energy traps triggering biexciton-exciton cascade and room-temperature photon bunching
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: time-resolved and super-resolution imaging evidence exciton migration; power dependence supports cascade mechanism

title: Role of fragility of the glass formers in the yielding transition under oscillatory shear
task: Understanding yielding transition age dependence across glass formers with varying fragility
key_results: Fragile glasses show yield stress increase with annealing below critical threshold; strong glasses show constant yield stress; universal behavior across metallic, molecular, granular, and silica glasses
comments: The modified mean-field elastoplastic model successfully unifies diverse glassy systems, though the microscopic mechanisms linking fragility to energy barriers warrant further experimental validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fragility determines how energy barriers change with annealing, controlling yield stress dependence
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: fragile glasses show larger yield stress changes with annealing than strong glasses

title: A general purposed machine learning interatomic potential for Mg-Al-Si-O system suitable for Earth materials at high pressure and temperature conditions
task: Developing machine learning interatomic potential for Mg-Al-Si-O system at extreme conditions
key_results: Pairwise Gaussian correction reduces energy error from 5.2 to 1.2 kJ/mol; predicted isochemical phase diagrams agree with experiments; solid-melt interfacial free energy anisotropy: 6% (periclase), 12% (forsterite); mean stress proxy for α-β quartz transition has ~17% error
comments: The r2SCAN functional choice is well-justified, but validation on independent experimental datasets beyond those used in training would strengthen transferability claims.

model_io.model: Machine learning interatomic potential (trained on r2SCAN DFT data with Gaussian correction)
inputs: atomic coordinates for Mg, Al, Si, O in >20 minerals and melts

hypotheses.hypothesis: ML potentials with Gaussian correction can accurately reproduce experimental phase relations in complex Earth materials
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: predicted isochemical phase diagrams show good agreement; error reduction demonstrates improved accuracy

title: Symmetry driven altermagnetic spin splitting in hexagonal CrTe from first principles
task: Identifying altermagnetic spin splitting in hexagonal CrTe without net magnetization
key_results: CrTe exhibits collinear magnetic order with significant spin splitting despite zero net magnetization; symmetry-protected spin polarization confirmed; reclassified as altermagnet rather than conventional antiferromagnet
comments: The DFT identification is solid, but experimental confirmation via spin-resolved ARPES or transport measurements is essential to validate the altermagnet classification.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CrTe exhibits altermagnetism: collinear spin-split bands with zero net magnetization due to symmetry protection
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: DFT reveals spin splitting in absence of net magnetization; symmetry-protected mechanism identified

title: Expanded Heisenberg Hamiltonians from a Mn/Bi DFT+U study on hexagonal antiferromagnet CaMn2Bi2: excitations and strain-controlled magnetic anisotropy switching
task: Constructing expanded Heisenberg Hamiltonians for antiferromagnetic Mn-pnictide with strain-tunable anisotropy
key_results: Standard Heisenberg model insufficient; extended model with local on-site magnetization terms accurately describes magnetic excitations; easy-plane anisotropy with in-plane axis switching via small strain; SOC-driven gap tunable by strain
comments: The Hubbard-model-inspired extended Hamiltonian is physically motivated, but quantitative strain thresholds for axis switching and implications for magneto-optical control deserve experimental validation.

model_io.model: DFT+U with SOC (Mn-3d and Bi-6p Hubbard corrections)
Extended Heisenberg Hamiltonian with on-site magnetization terms

hypotheses.hypothesis: Extended Heisenberg model with SOC-driven on-site terms accurately captures magnetic excitations; strain switches easy-axis orientation in Mn-pnictide
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: extended model reproduces DFT excitation spectra; strain-tunable anisotropy switching demonstrated via DFT+U

title: Spin polarized first principles study of electro-magnetic and optical properties of K2NaXI6 (X :Cr Fe) double halide perovskites
task: Predicting electromagnetic and optical properties of K2NaXI6 double halide perovskites using spin-polarized DFT
key_results: K2NaCrI6: 0.8 eV (spin-up) / 1.8 eV (spin-down) band gaps; K2NaFeI6: 3.2 eV (spin-up) / 0.4 eV (spin-down); ferromagnetic ordering with 3 µB (Cr) and 5 µB (Fe) total moments; n-type (Cr) and p-type (Fe) thermoelectric behavior
comments: TB-mBJ functional provides accurate gaps, but experimental validation of predicted band gaps and type conductivity across both compounds is crucial for device applications.

hypotheses.hypothesis: K2NaXI6 double perovskites exhibit spin-asymmetric band gaps and type-dependent thermoelectric properties suitable for multifunctional energy applications
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: TB-mBJ calculations reveal ferromagnetic ordering with large spin-splitting and contrasting thermoelectric doping

title: Mode localization in chiral periodic approximants of Fibonacci magnonic superlattices
task: Realizing mode localization and field-tunable flat bands in Fibonacci magnonic superlattices
key_results: Dispersionless flat bands at low frequencies with spatial localization in chiral nanomagnets; external magnetic field tunes flat-band frequency window; DMI and anisotropy modulation enable modal selectivity
comments: The flat-band tunability is experimentally promising, but micromagnetic simulation scope is limited to linear systems; nonlinear spin-wave interactions and damping effects at higher powers require further study.

model_io.model: Fibonacci superlattice approximant (chiral and nonchiral nanomagnets)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Aperiodic Fibonacci ordering with DMI modulation produces field-tunable flat bands with spatial mode localization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: flat bands emerge with controllable frequency window via external field; localization tied to material contrast

title: Dual-mode superconducting diode effect enabled by in-plane and out-of-plane magnetic field
task: Realization of dual-mode superconducting diode effect using orthogonal magnetic fields
key_results: Both out-of-plane and in-plane fields independently generate SDE with similar efficiency (~1-2%); two orders difference in operational field strength; independent temperature dependence for each mode
comments: The multi-directional symmetry breaking is elegantly demonstrated, though practical integration of fast polarity-switching and high-fidelity operations in a single device remains to be demonstrated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multiple symmetry-breaking orientations enable independent SDE operation modes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: orthogonal field components produce distinct operational regimes with independent temperature dependence

title: Magnetic clusters in the paramagnetic phase of a high-temperature ferromagnetic metal–organic framework
task: Characterizing magnetic cluster dynamics in paramagnetic phase of high-Tc ferrimagnetic metal-organic framework
key_results: Mesoscopic magnetic correlated clusters detected in paramagnetic regime above Tc; cluster dynamics tracked via nuclear moments in MHz range; similarity to magnetoelectronic phase segregation in manganites suggests common physics
comments: The unconventional magnetic transition and cluster formation mechanism merit closer scrutiny; whether clusters represent true equilibrium or kinetically trapped metastable states deserves clarification.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Paramagnetic phase of high-Tc MOF contains thermally-activated mesoscopic magnetic clusters with slow MHz-range dynamics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: NMR and FMR evidence slow relaxation mechanisms and cluster formation above Tc

title: Magnetically programmable surface acoustic wave filters: device concept and predictive modeling
task: Engineering magnetically programmable SAW filters via magnetoelastic coupling to spin waves
key_results: SAW attenuation of 52.0 dB/mm at 3.8 GHz achievable by programming internal magnetic state; stray-field interaction between Co/Ni islets shifts SW dispersion enabling frequency selectivity; energy conservation argument extended to finite-difference simulations
comments: The micromagnetic approach is thorough, but experimental realization of islet arrays and demonstration of programmable frequency tuning remain outstanding.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Stray-field-induced SW dispersion shifts enable magnetic-state-dependent SAW filtering without external field
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: micromagnetic modeling predicts 52 dB/mm attenuation variation with islet magnetic alignment

title: Bound states in the continuum and near-exceptional points in a reflection-based cavity-magnonic system
task: Realizing bound states in continuum and near-exceptional points in cavity-magnonic systems via reflection-based coupling
key_results: BICs with sharply enhanced group delay achieved via Fabry-Perot cavity-magnon interference; direction-selective coherent perfect absorption near PT-symmetric point; reflection-zero state in S-matrix enables non-Hermitian singularity access
comments: The reflection-based BIC realization is elegant and geometrically practical, though scalability to multi-mode systems and robustness to material losses at different frequencies warrant investigation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Interference between cavity mode and magnon produces reflection BICs with enhanced group delay; near-PT-symmetry enables direction-selective CPA
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: reflection zeros identified as BICs with sharp group delay peaks; directional loading achieves PT-like operating point

title: Skyrmion quantum diode prototype: bridging micromagnetic simulations and quantum models
task: Demonstrating skyrmion-based quantum diode for unidirectional qubit coupling
key_results: Unidirectional skyrmion transport via skyrmion Hall effect in 20-3 nm junctions; compatibility with flux-tunable quantum architectures demonstrated; anharmonicity signatures in skyrmion-qubit systems identified
comments: Bridging micromagnetics and quantum circuits is conceptually ambitious, but practical implementation challenges (skyrmion stability, qubit coherence in magnetic environment) and scaling to multi-qubit systems remain significant.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Skyrmion Hall effect enables unidirectional transport suitable for one-way quantum links between different qubit types
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: micromagnetic simulations demonstrate controlled unidirectional transport; quantum circuit model shows compatibility with superconducting architectures

title: Associations of continuous glucose monitor derived time in range and glycaemic variability with diet lifestyle and demographics
task: Assess associations between continuous glucose monitoring metrics and cardiometabolic health in non-diabetic individuals
key_results: Higher time in range (TIR 3.9–5.6 mmol/L) associated with lower HbA1c and OGTT glucose; TIR 3.9–5.6 achieved moderate discrimination for ASCVD 10-year risk (AUC = 0.75); sleep duration inversely correlated with mean glucose
comments: Large observational study (N=3,634) across three cohorts, but long-term health outcome data lacking; relevance of CGM metrics to actual disease prevention in euglycemic individuals remains uncertain.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Higher TIR is associated with better glycemic control markers
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: higher TIR 3.9–5.6 correlated with lower HbA1c and OGTT glucose

hypotheses.hypothesis: CGM metrics discriminate cardiovascular risk in non-diabetic individuals
hypotheses.result: Partially supported: moderate discrimination (AUC=0.75) but utility for health management unproven

title: Tumor-infiltrating immature innate lymphoid cells in colorectal cancer are biased toward ILC1/tissue-resident NK cell differentiation
task: Characterize innate lymphoid cell differentiation and dysfunction in colorectal cancer and peritoneal metastases
key_results: CRC tumors enriched for ILC1 and tissue-resident NK cells but depleted of ILC3; naïve ILCs transcriptionally biased toward ILC1/trNK differentiation; co-culture with OP9-DL1 induces ILC1/trNK-like differentiation
comments: Single-cell RNA-seq and functional assays provide mechanistic insight into ILC skewing, but sample size (11 patients scRNA-seq) is modest and generalizability to broader CRC populations unclear.

model_io.model: In vitro co-culture differentiation assay (24 patients)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tumor-infiltrating naïve ILCs are transcriptionally skewed toward ILC1/trNK differentiation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by scRNA-seq analysis showing ILC1/trNK transcriptional bias in nILCs

hypotheses.hypothesis: Co-culture with OP9-DL1 stromal cells induces nILC differentiation into ILC1/trNK cells
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: differentiation assays demonstrated intratumoral nILC conversion to ILC1/trNK-like phenotype
